seoTitle: What Is an Epic in Jira and How to Create One
metaDescription: Understand what an epic is in Jira and learn how to create one, attach child tasks, and track its progress on your roadmap or timeline.
intro: Large product goals can quickly get lost inside a cluttered backlog if there's no structure holding related tasks together. This guide explains what an epic is in Jira, how it acts as a container for stories and tasks, and how to create and track one from scratch.

steps.heading: Understand what an epic is
steps.body: An epic is a master container used to organize multiple related stories and tasks under a single high-level goal, rather than an issue type meant for day-to-day work.

steps.heading: Open the create issue window
steps.body: Click the Create button in the top navigation to launch the issue form where you'll set up your new epic.

steps.heading: Select Epic as the issue type
steps.body: Open the issue type dropdown, choose Epic, and confirm the project you're working in before continuing.

steps.heading: Fill in the epic details
steps.body: Type a clear summary title, define the scope of the work, and optionally assign a color tag to make the epic easy to spot on your board.

steps.heading: Link child tasks and track progress
steps.body: Once created, add child work items or stories to the epic, and check the timeline view to see how it renders against your project schedule.

faq.question: What is an epic in Jira used for?
faq.answer: An epic acts as a high-level container that groups related stories and tasks together under one larger goal, making it easier to track big initiatives.

faq.question: How do I create an epic in Jira?
faq.answer: Click Create, open the issue type dropdown, select Epic, fill in a summary and scope, and click Create to add it to your project.

faq.question: Can I link tasks to an epic after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, open the epic and use the add child work item option to link stories or tasks directly to it.

faq.question: How do I see an epic's progress?
faq.answer: Epics show up on the timeline view, where you can track completion progress and milestones alongside their linked child work.
